linkedin_profile.headline: Director of Commercial Strategy, Iconic Hotels & Resorts
linkedin_profile.about: A driven, results focused leader with a passion for people, performance, and purpose. Known for my creative energy and strategic mindset, I take a hands-on approach to leadership, motivating teams, inspiring collaboration, and delivering exceptional outcomes. With a strong commercial focus and sharp analytical skills, I consistently deliver impactful business results. I thrive in dynamic environments where innovation, accountability, and high standards shape success. Whether leading cross-functional teams or navigating complex challenges, I go the extra mile to turn ambition into achievement.

experience.title: Revenue Manager
experience.description: Internal promotion to Revenue manage the company’s Flagship hotel in London following tough competition from internal & external candidates. Responsible for a department of 15 team members, overseeing Rooms and Conference and Events, with assistance of 2 direct reports – Reservations Manager and Conference Planning Manager. Growing total Revenue from all POS in conjunction with Regional Director of Revenue & Regional General Manager while taking measured amount of risk. Implementing effective property strategies including pricing and inventory management to all market segments. Understanding the composition of the property profitability and use it to evaluate ideal business mix and lead time. Working closely with Regional Director of Revenue and Regional Sales Manager to set appropriate strategies in place to achieve budgeted targets. Identifying and sharing revenue trends and modify strategy accordingly, including channel mix, segmentation mix shift when required, LOS and lead time management and country mix. Chair weekly Revenue & Sales Meetings and Forecast meetings. Delivery of monthly financial reviews with CEO and board of directors. Budgeting for 2015 and 2016.

experience.title: Director Of Revenue Management
experience.description: Part of pre-opening team. Hotel opened following a multi-million refurbishment. Stand alone role. Part of the Senior leaders team. Hotel rewarded 5 stars by AA on the 3rd September, 2018 Responsible for maximizing revenue from all departments through setting effective revenue strategies in place with focus on delivering bottom line revenues. Repositioning of the hotel in luxury life style 5 star London market. Implement pricing strategies for a soft opening period, followed by a completely refurbished opening in Q3 2018 targeting the luxury market set. Pricing for all market segments, including assisting DOSM with setting corporate negotiations in place. Effectively managing all distribution channels with focus on gain versus cost of distribution. Targeted on achieving RGI goals post refurbishment in the 5-star market with an official comp set, a conference comp set and an aspirational comp set in place. Work closely with Reservations and Meetings and Events team on site, as well as communicating effectively to the central reservations team. Pricing for group business, including residential conferences concentrating on total revenue gain. Work closely with the Regional Director of Revenue, E-commerce Director and Commercial Director of the group to set appropriate strategies in place to achieve budgeted targets. Produce weekly forecast for rolling 3 months. Chair weekly Revenue & Sales Meetings and Forecast meetings. Delivery of monthly financial reviews with CEO and board of directors. Prepare yearly budgets. Revenue managed our sister property De Vere Cotswold Water Park from July – September 2017, mainly responsible for accommodation revenue. Exceeded budget every month since taking over this property and achieving first position in RGI in the competitor set. Revenue managed our sister property Principal York from September 2017-February 2018.

experience.title: Director Of Revenue Management
experience.description: Part of the pre-opening team, joined the leadership team to open the brand’s first international hotel (opened July 2019). Responsible for initial and 5-year budget projections to ensure target performance was achieved in the stabilised year. Responsible for all short- and long-term forecasts including Weekly Forecasts, 30 60 90 Forecasts, in the Month Forecast and Full Year Forecast. Brought in a Revenue Optimisation culture throughout the hotel’s departments to deliver room revenue projection. Implemented revenue strategies to progressively position the hotel as a RevPAR leader within its comp set. Implemented and ensured necessary revenue management disciplines were in place, providing dedicated attention to the property’s need to maximize total revenue through profitable market and channel segmentation mix, pattern management and ancillary spend. Conduct weekly Revenue, Sales and Marketing meetings with a focus on pricing, demand patterns, booking behaviour, pace, promotional activity, digital spend and conversion rates. Work with internal and external partners, enabling the property to ensure proper placement on web channels, assess need periods and upcoming promotional opportunities. Take ownership and present monthly financial reviews to Standard International Management & ownership team.

experience.title: Director Of Revenue Management
experience.description: Part of the pre-opening team, joined the leadership team to open the brand’s first international hotel (opened July 2019). Responsible for initial and 5-year budget projections to ensure target performance was achieved in the stabilised year. Responsible for all short- and long-term forecasts including Weekly Forecasts, 30 60 90 Forecasts, in the Month Forecast and Full Year Forecast. Brought in a Revenue Optimisation culture throughout the hotel’s departments to deliver £15m room revenue projection. Implemented revenue strategies to progressively position the hotel as a RevPAR leader within its comp set. Implemented and ensured necessary revenue management disciplines were in place, providing dedicated attention to the property’s need to maximize total revenue through profitable market and channel segmentation mix, pattern management and ancillary spend. experience.title: Regional Director Of Revenue Management
experience.description: Part of pre-opening team and Leadership team for Edinburgh and Glasgow. Responsible for opening and positioning Virgin Hotels in the UK and Europe as 5-star luxury lifestyle properties. Preparing Pre-opening and Post opening operating budgets for both properties. In charge of comprehensive sales and revenue projects, coordinating various aspects such as strategic planning, market analysis, business development, distribution strategy and building strong business partnerships. Set up Revenue & Reservation teams at both properties and trained the teams to work together to reach room revenue goal, while consistently upholding the brand’s culture and identity. Develop and implement innovative pricing strategies to maximise revenue and market share in all segments. Analyse cost of acquisition per segment and create awareness in Revenue and Sales department of the profitability of each segment. Drive strategies and create a revenue culture to capture and increase direct revenue contribution. Delivery of monthly financial reviews with Asset Manager, Ownership, and Head Office. Assisting with 5-year Proforma and revenue outlook for new developments for Virgin Hotels across UK & Europe.

experience.title: Chairman
experience.description: The formation of the Master Innholders was the first step taken by the WCI towards renewing its old links with innkeeping. In common with many other Livery Companies it had few links with the modern day industry. The award of membership is open to practicing hotel general managers by application each year. Full details can be viewed on the new members section. Candidates have to undergo a rigorous interview where they have to clearly demonstrate not only their innkeeping ability, but also continuing support for improving standards within the industry. With the full support of the Worshipful Company of Innholders and the Institute of Hospitality, the Master Innholders have slowly grown in number and have increased their influence and charitable activities within the hotel industry. The Master Innholders organises the annual General Managers’ Conference in London. The next conference will take place on 14th and 15th January 2013 at Grosvenor House. There are about 100 Master Innholders, all of whom are, or have been, prominent hoteliers, dedicated and committed to promoting best practice in the industry.

linkedin_profile.about:

I’ve partnered with executives and sales teams to deliver global enablement projects, events, and data-driven reporting solutions that improve performance and client engagement. Known for turning complex challenges into streamlined processes, I help teams focus on what they do best — growing pipelines, strengthening relationships, and achieving results.

experience.title: Hostess

Enhanced revenue per client through upselling and improved lead-source tracking for better sales insights. - Handled inbound sales enquiries, converting high-value prospects into bookings. - Maintained CRM accuracy with Opera PMS, ensuring consistent follow-up and account tracking. - Identified and executed upsell opportunities, improving revenue per client. - Optimised sales reporting processes by introducing lead-source tracking for better attribution.

experience.title: Sales Coordinator

Developed a post-event follow-up process that boosted repeat business and expanded corporate partnerships. - Managed a portfolio of corporate and B2B accounts, securing bookings and driving upsell opportunities. - Represented the brand at industry trade shows and networking events, strengthening client relationships. - Introduced a post-event follow-up process that improved repeat business and unlocked new opportunities. - Delivered tailored proposals, leveraging a consultative approach to align solutions with client goals.

experience.title: Sales Advisor

experience.title: Sales Project Coordinator

linkedin_profile.headline: Interim General Manager at Maroma, a Belmond Hotel
linkedin_profile.about:
Transformational luxury hospitality leader with over 20 years of experience as a General Manager across five-star hotels, serviced residences, and members’ clubs in London and internationally. I’ve led some of the capital’s most prestigious properties, including The Cadogan, A Belmond Hotel, 11 Cadogan Gardens and The Chelsea Townhouse (Iconic Luxury Hotels), and Jumeirah Carlton Tower. Known for delivering business turnarounds, developing award-winning teams, and executing major refurbishments and property launches, I thrive at the intersection of operations, brand storytelling, and ownership strategy. Whether relaunching heritage icons or launching new lifestyle concepts, I consistently drive operational excellence, guest satisfaction, and profitability. I’m now seeking my next leadership challenge in London — ideally managing a luxury hotel, branded residence, or lifestyle-led estate.

Total Experience: 26 years and 8 months

experience.title: Leisure Director
experience.description: Responsible for all sporting and related social activities at this famous private members club, comprising a 44 acre estate with 40 tennis Courts, Health and Fitness Centre, Indoor and Outdoor Swimming Pools, Cricket Pitch, 8 Bowls and Croquet Lawns and Grade II listed Clubhouse. Responsible for development and maintenance of all facilities, and ensuring professional operational standards and processes were applied in the context of a committee led private members club.

experience.title: General Manager London CityClubs
experience.description: Joined Champneys as GM at the Piccadilly Club but quickly promoted to Opening GM of the new flagship CityPoint Club in Moorgate. Successfully opened this 45,000 sq.ft fitness and spa facility, and then promoted to manage both Piccadilly and Moorgate Clubs.

experience.title: Manager - Peak Health Club And Spa
experience.description: Oversee operations at the Peak Health Club and Spa at the Jumeirah Carlton Tower Hotel at Knightsbridge and work on European Spa Development for Jumeirah

experience.title: Interim Revenue Manager
experience.location: Jumeirah Carlton Tower and Jumeirah Lowndes Hotels
experience.description: Responsible for all revenue management functions across both properties to ensure effective delivery of business strategy (forecasting, distribution, pricing, yield management)

experience.title: General Manager, Jumeirah Lowndes Hotel
experience.description: Responsible for the successful operation and financial performance of this luxury 88-bedroom boutique hotel in the heart of Knightsbridge (turnover £8.5m, FTE 70 Staff) Following a period of poor financial performance, appointed GM in 2010, and undertook a review of sales and marketing strategy and staffing structure in the last quarter of that year. From 2011 onwards the hotel has achieved budgeted profit every year since, with record profits in 2012, and record Rooms’ Revenue in 2016. - Completed refurbishment of all bedrooms in Q1 2014 on time and to budget - 35% growth in Net Profit between 2010 and 2013 (45% 2012 vs 2010) - Winner of 2012 Jumeirah Group “Market Share beyond Compare” (Best Year on Year Market Share Growth in the Jumeirah Group, and the 2013 Jumeirah Group “Sales Surge” Award (Best above budget profit performance in the Jumeirah Group) - 23% increase in Rooms Revenue growth from 2010 to 2013 - Customer Satisfaction Index grown from 752 in 2010 to 840 in 2017 - Refurbishment, rebranding and re-launch of Restaurant and Bar in 2011

experience.title: Interim General Manager - Grosvenor House Suites By Jumeirah Living
experience.description: Responsible for the successful operation and financial performance of this ultra-luxury property comprising 133 serviced apartments (198 bedrooms) on Park Lane. Chosen by Jumeirah following the departure of the previous GM to stabilise and refocus both colleagues and the business following a challenging 2014, and provide leadership to improve financial performance and service levels. During my tenure RevPAR grew 15.6% (vs Comp Set 0.4%), and the Customer Satisfaction Index grew 24 points.

experience.title: Pre-Opening Opening General Manager, Jumeirah Nanjing Hotel
experience.description: Successfully opened Jumeirah’s flagship hotel in China, comprising 260 keys, five F&B outlets, and full wellness facilities. - Customizing and executing comprehensive pre-opening plan - Project management and execution of snagging and commissioning processes - Maintained and developed owner relations in a challenging government environment - Onboarding, training and developing 345 colleagues to brand standards. - Hotel successfully opened on 8th September 2018 - Jumeirah Nanjing finished with the highest CSI (Customer Satisfaction Index) score in the Jumeirah portfolio at the end of 2018

experience.title: Interim General Manager Jumeirah Carlton Tower Hotel
experience.description: Given full operational responsibility for this 216 bedroom five red star hotel with three F&B outlets, C&E space and comprehensive wellness facilities with P&L responsibility for turnover of £40m (£27m rooms, £9m F&B, £4m OOD) in January 2019, in addition to my role at Jumeirah Lowndes Hotel. - In April 2019, it was announced that the property would be closing for a full refurbishment on 1st September 2019. - Successfully planned and executed the closure of the hotel whilst maintaining service standards and delivering an above budget financial performance for the year.

experience.title: General Manager
experience.organization: 11 Cadogan Gardens, The Chelsea Townhouse and The Apartments by 11 Cadogan Gardens
experience.description: I was fortunate to oversee these three amazing properties, and an equally amazing team! 11 Cadogan Gardens and the Chelsea Townhouse are the only two Relais & Chateaux properties in London, flanked by the six luxury apartments of the Apartments by 11 Cadogan Gardens, and peacefully situated around a leafy garden square, yet just seconds from the Kings Road, Sloane Square and Sloane Street

experience.title: Interim General Manager

During my tenure the hotel achieved 2 Michelin Key status, was voted 6th Best London Hotel in the Condé Nast Traveller Readers Awards, and secured it’s highest LQA Audit of 90%, ranking it 7th out of the 72 participating London Hotels.

linkedin_profile.headline: Executive Chef at The Winding Stair

experience.title: Executive Chef
experience.description: The Market Café is a Modern British Bistro with a large focus on weekend brunches. With a restaurant that can seat up to 110 people, with average dinner covers of around 50-60 and weekend brunch covers of 300-500 people this was an extremely busy kitchen. With a brigade of 7 chefs we managed turn it from a food GP of 36% down to 29% in the space of about four months. With a weekly food turnover now of £15,000-£20,000 it ha shown an increase of 20% from the same times in 2014-2015. Les Nenettes is a small French Bistro, opened in February, located in Hackney open all day serving Brunch, Lunch and Dinner. With seating of 40 inside and 25 outside with an extremely small kitchen we have got this up and running in under 3 months. A brigade of 5 chefs and 2 KPs. As Executive Chef I was responsible for the day to day running of both restaurants and more recently, designing and opening the Les Nenettes Restaurant.

experience.title: Head Chef
experience.description: Eat 17 Restaurant is a neighbourhood bistro with a large clientele of regular diners. With seating of around 70 covers Eat 17 does on average around 1300 covers a week. Open for lunch and dinner Monday-Friday, and brunch, lunch and dinner on the weekends, it is a fast paced restaurant while still maintaining its high standards in food and service. As the Head Chef my responsibilities include, rota, wage costings, menu design and implementation, menu costings and all aspects of health and safety.

experience.title: Executive Chef
experience.description: The Oslo Bar and Restaurant is a busy bar/restaurant/live music venue. It is based in Hackney, East London and is a joint restaurant/bar and live music venue. I was employed as the Executive Chef to oversee the complete overhaul of the Oslo kitchen. In early July we created a new plan and direction for the Oslo kitchen. After a reorganisation of the kitchen and surrounding areas to improve efficiency, we focused on staff training and taking the food back to basics with 90% of the items being made in house. With regular food training, on tasting, allergens, coffee training, wine training and an in house 10 module health, hygiene and safety program we re-launched the Oslo kitchen. The restaurant has a maximum seating of 80pax and is open for lunch and dinner 7 days a week. With a focus on fresh food, using local suppliers where possible, such as the E5 Bakehouse, Fin and Flounder & Cream Lab. The restaurant is marketed as an inviting gastro pub, serving fresh, homemade comfort food. With daily changing specials, a very busy Sunday lunch and a packed restaurant on music nights, the Oslo has transformed into a staple East London dining choice.
